summary: Stage Payment Flow Testing - Complete. Fixed critical route ordering bug in backend (payment-requests endpoint was shadowed by parameterized route). All 17 backend tests pass. E2E flow tested: Site Engineer requests payment -> Planning approves -> Accounts processes. UI tests verified all three dashboards.

fixed.issue: Route was being matched by /api/work-orders/{work_order_id} returning 404
fixed.fix: Moved payment-requests route before the parameterized {work_order_id} route

seed_data_creation: Used existing test work order WO-TEST-001 (wo_197983e92fd8) with 3 stages - all now marked as paid after testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Stage payment flow is fully functional. Work order WO-TEST-001 has all 3 stages paid (Foundation ₹15,000, Wall Construction ₹25,000, Finishing ₹10,000, Total ₹50,000). The route ordering fix in server.py was critical - always place specific routes (like /work-orders/payment-requests) before parameterized routes (like /work-orders/{work_order_id}).

site_engineer_dashboard.GET /api/site-engineer/my-projects: PASS - Returns projects assigned to site engineer
site_engineer_dashboard.GET /api/site-engineer/work-orders: PASS - Returns work orders with stages
site_engineer_dashboard.PATCH /api/work-orders/{wo_id}/stages/{s: PASS - Creates payment request, notifies Planning

planning_board.GET /api/work-orders/payment-requests: PASS - Returns pending payment requests (Fixed route ordering)
planning_board.PATCH /api/work-orders/{wo_id}/stages/{s: PASS - Rejects payment back to site engineer

accounts_board.GET /api/accounts/dashboard: PASS - Returns metrics including stage_payments count and total
accounts_board.GET /api/accounts/pending-payments: PASS - Returns all pending payments including stage payments
accounts_board.GET /api/accounts/pending-payments?payme: PASS - Filters to stage payments only

e2e_flow.stage2_wall_construction: PASS - Full flow tested (start -> complete -> request -> approve -> process)
e2e_flow.stage3_finishing: PASS - Full flow tested (start -> complete -> request -> approve -> process)
e2e_flow.work_order_completion: PASS - Work order marked as completed when all stages paid

site_engineer_dashboard.page_loads: PASS - Orange theme navigation with Site Engineer branding
site_engineer_dashboard.tabs: PASS - Projects and Work Orders tabs functional
site_engineer_dashboard.work_order_display: PASS - Shows WO-TEST-001 with PAYMENT STAGES section
site_engineer_dashboard.stage_actions: PASS - Start Work, Mark Complete, Request Payment buttons displayed correctly
site_engineer_dashboard.payment_request_dialog: PASS - Dialog shows work order details, stage amount, remarks input

planning_board.page_loads: PASS - Indigo theme with Planning Board branding
planning_board.dashboard_metrics: PASS - New Projects, Awaiting Approval, Working, Completed cards
planning_board.payment_requests_alert: PASS - Purple alert shown when payment requests exist
planning_board.payment_dialog: PASS - Shows work order, stage, amount, approve/reject buttons

accounts_board.page_loads: PASS - Emerald theme with Accounts Board branding
accounts_board.dashboard_metrics: PASS - Material, Labour, Procurement, Stage Payments, Total Pending cards
accounts_board.tabs: PASS - All, Material, Labour, Procurement, Stage tabs functional
accounts_board.stage_tab: PASS - Shows stage payments with Process button
accounts_board.process_dialog: PASS - Shows stage details with Confirm Payment button

mocked_apis.none: All APIs are real - no mocked endpoints

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: FastAPI route matching order - the parameterized route /work-orders/{work_order_id} was defined before /work-orders/payment-requests, causing 'payment-requests' to be treated as a work_order_id
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction: GET /api/work-orders/payment-requests returned 404 'Work order not found' because it matched /work-orders/{work_order_id} with work_order_id='payment-requests'
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Moved /work-orders/payment-requests route definition before /work-orders/{work_order_id} in server.py. Also removed duplicate definition of the endpoint that was later in the file.
